meta.name: Visium analysis & visualization (scanpy)
meta.description: The canonical scanpy Visium walkthrough: QC → count/gene filtering → normalize → log1p → highly variable genes (Seurat, 2000) → PCA → neighbors → UMAP (2D, plus a 3-component UMAP in obsm['X_umap_3d']) → Leiden (obs['clusters']) → per-cluster marker genes (t-test). For full-transcriptome Visium H&E sections; the fixed count thresholds are Visium-scale and will over-filter targeted panels.
meta.provenance: Adapted from the scanpy 'Analysis and visualization of spatial transcriptomics data' tutorial (Visium mouse-brain section).

readme: End-to-end reproduction of scanpy's Visium basic-analysis tutorial. Drops spots outside `min_counts`–`max_counts` total counts and genes seen in fewer than `min_cells` spots, selects `n_top_genes` highly variable genes (Seurat flavor, on log data), then runs the standard PCA → neighbors → UMAP → Leiden path (also computing a 3-component UMAP in `obsm['X_umap_3d']` for the 3D scatter view) and ranks marker genes per cluster. Clusters land in `obs[cluster_key]`; markers in `uns['rank_genes_groups']`. The tutorial's mitochondrial QC (`qc_vars=['mt']` and the `pct_counts_mt` cutoff) needs a pre-set `var['mt']` mask and a manual boolean subset, so it is omitted here. Count thresholds assume full-transcriptome Visium data — not for targeted panels such as Xenium.

params.name: min_counts
params.tooltip: drop spots with fewer than this many total counts

params.name: max_counts
params.tooltip: drop spots with more than this many total counts

params.name: n_top_genes
params.tooltip: highly variable genes to keep for PCA

params.name: cluster_key
params.tooltip: obs column the Leiden clustering writes and marker ranking groups by

params.name: method
params.tooltip: differential-expression test used to rank markers
